steps.title: Install Library
steps.tip: Use pip3 if pip points to Python 2. Virtual environments keep dependencies clean

steps.title: Import Modules
steps.tip: whois for WHOIS data, socket for DNS resolution. Both are essential for recon automation

steps.title: WHOIS Lookup
steps.instruction: WHOIS lookup: w = whois.whois(domain); print(w.registrar, w.creation_date)
steps.tip: Not all WHOIS fields are present for all domains. Always check for None values

steps.title: DNS Resolution
steps.tip: This returns only one IP. For multiple IPs, use socket.getaddrinfo()

steps.title: Reverse DNS
steps.tip: Reverse DNS often reveals hosting provider or CDN information

steps.title: Error Handling
steps.instruction: Wrap each in try/except to handle lookup failures
steps.tip: WHOIS may timeout, DNS may fail, reverse DNS may not exist. Handle each gracefully

steps.title: Format Output
steps.instruction: Format output with clear headers and save to file
steps.tip: Use consistent formatting across all output. Make it professional enough for a client report
